notes: Verified via PE import table analysis that libSkiaSharp.dll gained hard d3d12.dll and D3DCOMPILER_47.dll imports in 3.119.0-preview.1.2 (absent in 3.118.0-preview.2.3). The dependency persists through 3.119.2 (latest stable) and main branch. However, the actual DLL load crash requires a Windows system without D3D12 (e.g., Windows 7 or certain Windows 10 configurations), which is not available on this macOS ARM64 host. The nanoserver build variant has SUPPORT_DIRECT3D=false and no d3d12 imports, confirming the flag is toggleable. The main branch build.cake still defaults SUPPORT_DIRECT3D to true with no /DELAYLOAD flags.
blockers: Requires Windows system without d3d12.dll (e.g., Windows 7 or Windows 10 without D3D12 drivers) to observe the actual native library load crash — current environment is macOS ARM64
Docker on macOS cannot run Windows containers

reproductionSteps.description: Create console app with reporter's SkiaSharp version 3.119.0-preview.1.2
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3267 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3267 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.0-preview.1.2
reproductionSteps.output:
The template "Console App" was created successfully.
PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp' version '3.119.0-preview.1.2' added.

filesCreated.description: Console app that loads native SkiaSharp library via SKFontManager.Default to trigger DLL dependency check

reproductionSteps.description: Build and run console app on macOS ARM64 — native library loads successfully (macOS uses libSkiaSharp.dylib, no d3d12 dependency)
reproductionSteps.output:
Build succeeded. 0 Warning(s) 0 Error(s)
Before
After - FontManager family count: 180
SUCCESS: test completed

reproductionSteps.description: Analyze PE import table of Windows x64 libSkiaSharp.dll from NuGet package 3.119.0-preview.1.2 — confirms hard d3d12.dll dependency
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import pefile; pe = pefile.PE('$HOME/.nuget/packages/skiasharp.nativeassets.win32/3.119.0-preview.1.2/runtimes/win-x64/native/libSkiaSharp.dll'); [print(e.dll.decode()) for e in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT]"
reproductionSteps.output:
ole32.dll
FONTSUB.dll
USER32.dll
d3d12.dll
D3DCOMPILER_47.dll
KERNEL32.dll

reproductionSteps.description: Analyze PE import table of Windows x64 libSkiaSharp.dll from NuGet package 3.118.0-preview.2.3 (last known good) — confirms NO d3d12.dll dependency
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import pefile; pe = pefile.PE('$HOME/.nuget/packages/skiasharp.nativeassets.win32/3.118.0-preview.2.3/runtimes/win-x64/native/libSkiaSharp.dll'); [print(e.dll.decode()) for e in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT]"

reproductionSteps.description: Analyze PE import table of Windows x64 libSkiaSharp.dll from NuGet package 3.119.2 (latest stable) — d3d12.dll dependency still present
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import pefile; pe = pefile.PE('$HOME/.nuget/packages/skiasharp.nativeassets.win32/3.119.2/runtimes/win-x64/native/libSkiaSharp.dll'); [print(e.dll.decode()) for e in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT]"

reproductionSteps.description: Analyze PE import table of Windows x64 libSkiaSharp.dll from main branch (externals-download) — d3d12.dll dependency still present on main
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import pefile; pe = pefile.PE('output/native/windows/x64/libSkiaSharp.dll'); [print(e.dll.decode()) for e in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT]"

reproductionSteps.description: Verify nanoserver variant has no d3d12.dll dependency (SUPPORT_DIRECT3D=false) — confirms the flag works
reproductionSteps.command: python3 -c "import pefile; pe = pefile.PE('output/native/nanoserver/x64/libSkiaSharp.dll'); [print(e.dll.decode()) for e in pe.DIRECTORY_ENTRY_IMPORT]"

versionResults.notes: PE import analysis confirms no d3d12.dll dependency. Cannot runtime-test on macOS (need Windows without D3D12).
PE import analysis confirms d3d12.dll is a hard dependency. macOS app runs fine (no d3d12 on macOS). Cannot trigger the DLL load crash without Windows.
PE import analysis confirms d3d12.dll dependency persists in latest stable. Cannot runtime-test the crash on macOS.
PE import analysis of downloaded Windows binary confirms d3d12.dll dependency persists on main. Build config still has SUPPORT_DIRECT3D defaulting to true.
